target.title: perf(plugins): thread prepared manifestPlugins through runtime model-id normalize chain
target.body:
## Summary

Extends the prepared-manifest-metadata threading from #82814 into the runtime plugin-normalize branch so callers that already carry prepared `manifestPlugins` skip the residual `installs.json` slot-or-load disk walk in `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies`. Closes the gap that #82814's measurements left visible (Kova `agent-cold-warm-message` was at 28 metadata scans per cold+warm sample post-#82814).

Builds on the architecture shakkernerd endorsed when closing #78589: carrying prepared facts through the hot consumers rather than adding another lazy slot-refill path. Does not touch `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies` itself.

## Why

`#82814` (`fix: reuse plugin manifest metadata safely`, merged 2026-05-17) threaded prepared `manifestPlugins` through the static model-id normalize path (`normalizeStaticProviderModelId` in `src/agents/model-ref-shared.ts`) so consumers stop consulting the `current-plugin-metadata-snapshot` single-slot handoff in `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies`. That eliminated most of the hot-path `installs.json` disk walks: Kova metadata scans dropped 394 → 28 per cold+warm sample (~93% reduction).

The remaining 28 scans come from the runtime plugin-normalize branch. `src/agents/model-selection-normalize.ts` already receives `options.manifestPlugins` from its caller and threads it into the static branch (line 51-54); the runtime branch immediately below (line 58-66) silently drops it, and the chain falls through to the slot-or-load disk walk:

\`\`\`
src/agents/model-selection-normalize.ts:59
  → normalizeProviderModelIdWithRuntime(...)         (no plugins param)
    → normalizeProviderModelIdWithPlugin(params)     (no plugins param)
      → normalizeProviderModelIdWithManifest(params) (params.plugins undefined)
        → loadManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies   (slot-or-load disk walk)
\`\`\`

`normalizeProviderModelIdWithManifest` already honors `params.plugins` to short-circuit:

\`\`\`ts
function loadManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies(params) {
  if (params.plugins) {
    return collectManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies(params.plugins);  // prepared path
  }
  const { snapshot, cacheable } = resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies(params);  // slot-or-load
  // ...
}
\`\`\`

The intermediate functions just never accepted a `plugins` field. This PR adds it as an additive optional param and forwards `options?.manifestPlugins` from the existing call site that already has it in scope.

## How

Three surgical edits:

1. **`src/agents/provider-model-normalization.runtime.ts`** — add optional `plugins?: readonly Pick<PluginManifestRecord, \"modelIdNormalization\">[]` to `normalizeProviderModelIdWithRuntime`'s params. The body just forwards `params` unchanged to `normalizeProviderModelIdWithPlugin`, so no behavioral change beyond the type signature.

2. **`src/plugins/provider-runtime.ts`** — add the same optional `plugins?` field to `normalizeProviderModelIdWithPlugin`'s params. The existing `normalizeProviderModelIdWithManifest(params)` call already forwards everything, so `plugins` flows straight through to where it's honored.

3. **`src/agents/model-selection-normalize.ts:59`** — at the runtime call site, thread `options?.manifestPlugins` into the runtime call. Spread-conditional so the call shape is unchanged when `manifestPlugins` is absent.

Net diff: +6 LOC across three source files (plus tests and changelog).

## Relationship to the previously closed PR #78589

`#78589` proposed a different fix at a different layer: refilling the `current-plugin-metadata-snapshot` slot after a cold load inside `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies`. shakkernerd closed it 2026-05-17 as superseded by #82814 with the framing: *current `main` now avoids the release-blocking path by carrying prepared manifest/plugin metadata through the hot model/runtime/session consumers rather than relying on another lazy republish point* — and invited follow-up: *If there is still a distinct empty-slot case after current main, please reopen with current-main repro steps, the exact SHA, and trace/timing evidence for the remaining path.*

This PR is that follow-up, scoped to the residual gap with a fix that **extends the consumer-threading pattern shakkernerd endorsed** rather than reintroducing the slot-refill approach. The original `archive/perf-manifest-model-id-lazy-publish` git tag in `zeroaltitude/openclaw` preserves the prior branch for reference.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Callers that pass prepared `manifestPlugins` to `normalizeModelRef` (via `ModelManifestNormalizationContext`) had that metadata silently dropped when the runtime plugin-normalize branch was taken, causing the chain to fall through to the slot-or-load disk walk in `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies` even though prepared metadata was available in the caller's scope.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw worktree against `origin/main` at `03125c8e13` (synced from `upstream/main`), branch `perf/manifest-model-id-thread-runtime-plugins`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json`
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-selection.plugin-runtime.test.ts src/agents/model-selection-manifest-workspace.test.ts src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts src/plugins/manifest-model-id-normalization.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/provider-model-normalization.runtime.ts src/agents/model-selection-normalize.ts src/agents/model-selection.plugin-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**: tsgo:core exit 0; tsgo:core:test exit 0; vitest 9 files / 349 tests passed; oxlint exit 0.
- **Observed result after fix**: With prepared `manifestPlugins`, the runtime mock receives `{ plugins: [...] }` and the production chain hands them through to `normalizeProviderModelIdWithManifest`, where the existing `params.plugins` short-circuit in `loadManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies` takes the prepared path and skips `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies` entirely. Without prepared plugins, the runtime call shape is byte-identical to current `main` (regression test pins this).
- **What was not tested**: I have not run the full Kova `agent-cold-warm-message` repro on a deployed-style mock provider environment to measure the 28 → ~3 scan reduction in production scale. The mechanism is identical to #82814's: when `params.plugins` is present, `loadManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies` returns immediately from `collectManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies(params.plugins)` without invoking `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies`, so the savings scale 1:1 with how often the runtime plugin-normalize branch is hit on cold-slot turns. Maintainers with the Kova harness can re-run that scenario; happy to add that measurement if you can point me at the canonical fixture.

## Tests

Added in `src/agents/model-selection.plugin-runtime.test.ts`:

- `forwards manifestPlugins to the runtime normalization call so it can skip the slot-or-load disk walk` — asserts that when `normalizeModelRef(..., { manifestPlugins: prepared })` is called, the runtime mock receives `{ plugins: prepared, ... }`.
- `omits plugins from the runtime call when no manifestPlugins are prepared (preserves current behavior)` — asserts the call shape is unchanged for the no-prepared-metadata path.

All existing test cases in the affected files continue to pass.

## Verification

- `tsgo:core`: clean
- `tsgo:core:test`: clean
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs <5 affected files>`: 9 files / 349 tests passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs <4 affected source files>`: 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `git diff --check`: clean
- Branched from `origin/main` (= `upstream/main` at `03125c8e13`); no rebase needed at submission.
